title: What does the role of Iranian supreme leader entail?
description: Following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in US-Israeli strikes against Iran, a council of 88 Shiite clerics are set to appoint a new supreme leader. The long-running favourite is the late supreme leader's son, but he faces reservations: the supreme leadership was never supposed to become a family dynasty like the Shahs. But what does the supreme leader actually do?

title: A look at who the frontrunners are for Iran's supreme leader position
description: As the funeral procession of the late Ali Khamenei has been postponed amid continued US-Israeli strikes, the council of clerics charged with choosing his successor are yet to make an announcement, despite whispers that a decision may already have been taken. This after Israel said early on March 3rd that any new appointment would be immediately targeted for assassination.

title: Spain's Sanchez defies Trump trade threat, compares Iran war to ‘Russian roulette’
description: Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez doubled down Wednesday, criticising US and Israeli military actions in Iran and refusing to bow to trade pressure from Washington. In a defiant televised address, Sanchez warned that the escalating conflict in the Middle East was akin to “playing Russian roulette” with millions of lives.

title: South Korean stock market plunges over potential Iran-related energy crisis
description: In a shock for South Korea, the country's KOSPI index dropped by over 12 percent Wednesday in its worst sell off ever. Share prices took a dive as investors panicked about a potential energy crisis if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. South Korea, alongside many Asian countries, is especially dependent on Middle Eastern oil, making its businesses vulnerable to the geopolitics of the region.

title: Countries work to secure safe passage in the Strait of Hormuz
description: A container ship was struck off the coast of Oman on March 3rd while transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claim to have full control of the key waterway for global oil transit, but traffic has currently come to a halt following drone attacks on ships.

title: Unilateral US strikes on Iran show "power consolidation around the presidential authority"

This took place ahead of debates ahead of a vote of a war powers resolution, which gives lawmakers an opportunity to demand congressional approval before any further attacks are carried out.

title: Iranian strikes on Israel decrease, although cause remains "unclear"
description: Israel is seeing a decline in launches from Iran as the campaign enters its fifth day, although reasons for it remain unclear. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky has more from Jerusalem.

title: "US lawmakers' efforts to curb Trump's ability to continue war almost sure to fail"
description: Lawmakers in the U.S. Senate are set to vote on March 4th on a bipartisan war powers resolution aiming to stop the military campaign against Iran and require that any ​hostilities against it ‌be authorised by Congress. This comes as Trump faces little voter support for the war. But lawmakers' efforts to curb Trump's ability to "goad alone with Israel in this war against Iran are almost sure to fail", warns FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.

title: Iran war opens cracks within Trump's MAGA base
description: President Trump has launched into a war while he’s facing some of the lowest approval ratings of his political career. A recent poll showed that just one in four Americans approved of his attack on Iran. But it’s not just the general American public: The war is also beginning to show some cracks in support from his die-hard “Make American Great Again” base.

title: Broadest evacuation order for Lebanon issued by Israeli army
description: The Israeli military ordered the ​evacuation of ‌a swathe of southern Lebanon on March 4th, asking residents to move north. This evacuation is the broadest evacuation order yet in this new conflict. Meanwhile, Syria has closed its border with Lebanon after a warning it would be targeted by Israe. FRANCE 24's Renee Davis has more from Beirut.

title: US plans for Iran remain unclear as it defends "pre-emptive" strikes

Stated US motivations have also shifted over time, from intervening on the behalf anti-regime protesters in January to "preemptively' striking the country.

title: "Everything is speculation" over Khamenei succession but son set as favourite
description: Iran announced that a state funeral for supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was postponed after it was meant to be held on March 4th. This comes as the late supreme leader's successor is set to be appointed, with his son expected to take on the role. But "everything about this nomination is speculation", warns FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert.

Finally, a story about mini (and maxi) pet pigs.

The decision was made for patient safety, but also to reassure those for whom the sirens are a source of anxiety. At least 11 have died and hundreds have been left injured from strikes on Israel.

It comes as the region sees more attacks targeting US assets amid Iran’s retaliation to US-Israeli strikes.

title: Us-Israeli strikes on Iran intensify as war enters fifth day
description: The US and Israel intensified strikes on Iran on March 3rd and 4th. Since the start of the conflict, hundreds of people have been killed in the country, including over 160 students who died in an attack on a girl school in the south of the country. The admiral in charge of US operation revealed that the first 24 hours of strikes were nearly double the shock and awe tactics used during the Iraq war in 2003.

title: Air travel industry faces worse crisis since pandemic
description: The conflict in the Middle East between the US, Israel and Iran has set off a cascade of travel disruption around the entire planet. Airspace across much of the region remains closed, with Gulf state airlines from the major global hubs continuing to cancel flights. However, respite came on March 2nd for at least a few of the tens of thousands stranded, with a few international airlines resuming a small number of flights to repatriate those who'd

title: Trump threatens Spain with trade embargo for criticism of Iran campaign
description: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez has condemned what he described as the US and Israel's unilateral military action in Iran, adding that the move contributes to “a more hostile and uncertain international order.” Now, in contrast to other EU nations, Spain is refusing use of jointly operated military bases on its territory to attack Iran. In response, US President Donald Trump has brandished economic punishment.

title: France deploys its aircraft carrier to protect Middle East allies
description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced on March 3rd the deployment of additional military resources, including the aircraft carrier Charles De Gaulle and its escort, to help protect his allies facing the consequences of the war in the Middle East. The flagship of the French fleet is interrupting its planned deployment in the North Atlantic to head for the Mediterranean.

title: Israel expands strikes in Lebanon and issues new evacuation warnings
description: Israel and Hezbollah have been trading tit for tat strikes for a second day. According to a Lebanese health ministry official, 52 people have been killed and more than 150 wounded due to Israeli attacks . Thousands of civilians have been displaced, and on March 3rd the Israeli military issued dozens of new evacuation warnings for areas it said were near Hezbollah's facilities.

title: Republican concern over effect of Iran war on party popularity ahead of midterms
description: As the Iran war enters its fifth day, the Trump administration is facing growing criticism. After US Secretary of State Marco Rubio hinted at Israel having forced the US's hand to strike Iran, MAGA backlash grew as they are "isolationists and often anti-Israel", FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani explains. With the midterms approaching, some Republicans worry this will not help party popularity.

title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Tehran as war enters fifth day
description: The United States and Israel hit Iran's capital and other cities in multiple airstrikes on March 4, the fifth day of the war with Iran. Israel targeted the Iranian leadership and security forces as the Islamic Republic responded with missile barrages and drone attacks on Israel and across the region.

title: The Lebanese ambassador to France speaks to FRANCE 24
description: Israeli strikes hit a Beirut hotel and residential complex in Lebanon's east, Lebanese state media said on March 4th, as Israel announced broad strikes in response to Hezbollah missile and drone launches. "We should have a conference in France to help the Lebanese army to continue its plans" to disarm Hezbollah, Rabih El Chaer, Lebanese ambassador to France, tells FRANCE 24.

title: France deploys military assets to Mediterranean amid war in the Mideast
description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Tuesday ordered a nuclear-powered aircraft carrier from the Baltic Sea to the Mediterranean to help protect allied assets as war in the Middle East showed no signs of de-escalating. Macron also noted that France has defence agreements with several Gulf states as well as commitments to Jordan and Iraq.

title: Korean stocks suffer worst crash ever as Iran war hits chipmakers
description: South Korea's main stock index, the KOSPI, lost 12 percent on Wednesday, sending retail investors into a panic as fears of energy shortages due to the war in Iran battered shares in chipmakers and industrial giants. Meanwhile, Donald Trump says the US will insure oil tankers transiting the Persian Gulf as operating costs skyrocket, and the travel industry suffers as key Middle East hubs remain out of commission.

title: China to unveil next five years' priorities at 'Two Sessions' annual political conclave
description: China on Wednesday kicked off its annual "Two Sessions" political meetings during which the country's leadership will announce its annual growth target, defence budget and new five-year plan. The National People's Congress will ratify new laws decided by China's Communist Party leadership.

title: Austal USA launches future USNS Solomon Atkinson in Alabama
description:
Austal USA launched the future USNS Solomon Atkinson the company’s first ship of 2026 and its second Navy Towing, Salvage, and Rescue Ship.

title: Houthis express solidarity with Iran but do not launch retaliatory attacks—yet
description: Houthi leader Abdulmalik al Houthi has delivered speeches in support of the Islamic Republic of Iran since the start of the US-Israeli military campaign, but he has not yet announced specific retaliatory military action. Instead, the Houthis have officially called for mass demonstrations and increased media activity in support of the regime. However, officials from the Iran-backed group have told media outlets that it will resume attacks against

title: US-Israeli campaign hits Iranian regime’s military and repression apparatus, regime forms leadership council (March 1 up
description: The joint US and Israeli operation entered its second day, with American forces targeting command-and-control centers, Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) headquarters, air defense systems, ballistic missile sites, and Iran’s naval forces. In parallel, Israeli strikes concentrated on targets in Tehran, including key pillars of the regime’s repression apparatus, as well as ballistic missile sites and launchers. title: Iranian Kurdish groups unite against Tehran regime, Iraqi militias threaten Kurdistan Region
description:
Five Iranian Kurdish opposition groups have announced a coalition aimed at opposing Iran’s regime. The alliance, unveiled at a joint press conference on February 22, includes the PDKI, PJAK, PAK, Komala, and the Khabat Organization. The move comes amid heightened US-Iran tensions and renewed threats from the Iranian-backed Iraqi militia Kataib Hezbollah, which warned the Kurdistan Region against cooperating with “foreign forces.”

title: Palestinian factions mourn death of Iran’s leader, stop short of threatening retaliation
description:
Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and other Palestinian terrorist organizations issued statements mourning the deaths of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and the Tehran regime’s other senior officials. While some groups made vague mentions of a response to the killings, none explicitly said they would retaliate against Israel or the United States.

title: CIA tracked Iranian leaders for months ahead of attacks that began with 3 strikes in 60 seconds
description: The eventual barrage of U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran came so quickly that they were nearly simultaneous, an Israeli military official said Sunday.

title: ‘America First’ weapons-sales policy favors arming Taiwan quickly — in theory
description: But experts say the new U.S. strategy is unlikely to raise or lower the odds of Washington actually coming to help Taiwan if China attacks.

title: 🔴 Live: Iran says won't negotiate with US, can 'continue the war'
Iran to begin three-day funeral rites for late Ayatollah Khamenei in Tehran
description: The state funeral for Iran’s late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is set to take place on Wednesday evening in Tehran and will last three days. The 86‑year‑old former leader was killed on Saturday in US-Israeli air strikes. Israel said that any successor to him would be "a target".

title: War in Middle East live: Iran says won’t negotiate with US, can ‘continue the war’
description: A senior adviser to late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei said Wednesday that Iran will not negotiate with the US and is ready to “continue the war”. Iran’s Revolutionary Guards claimed “total control” of the Strait of Hormuz as Israel warned it would assassinate any successor to late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: Hundreds of drones target Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE amid Iran war
description: IRGC says the attacks are among its first powerful steps as it continues strikes on US assets in Gulf countries.

title: Analyst says interest in Epstein files plummeted after war on Iran launched
description: Republican US Congressman Thomas Massie has said the war won’t make the Epstein files go away.

title: Will the US put ‘boots on the ground’ in Iran?
description: Trump hasnt ruled out ground troops in Iran, but experts expect targeted special ops over an invasion.

title: What we know on day five of US-Israeli attacks on Iran
description: Strikes have hit nuclear-related facilities in Tehran, while Iran has launched hundreds of missiles and drones.

title: IRGC says Iran in ‘complete control’ of Strait of Hormuz amid Trump threats
description: One-fifth of the global oil is shipped through the strait, which remains effectively closed amid US-Israeli war on Iran.

title: Trump says Iran leader alternatives mostly killed
description: Trump told reporters that most of the people he had considered as potential future leaders for Iran are now dead.

title: How long can Israel sustain a military conflict with Iran?
